query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "shuttermuse: capture-time photography guidance with mllms" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on traditional image annotation using coupled dictionary learning and marginalized loss functions, which is a distinct construct from the user's question about extending an MLLM-based photography guidance system ("ShutterMuse"). It fails the inclusion criteria because it does not measure the same mechanism (MLLM guidance), study the same domain (real-time photography capture guidance), or provide a canonical baseline for MLLM-based image interaction, representing an off-domain
